test: GLM-5.2-NVFP4 DSA-sparse TP=4 on LATEST vLLM (dev207+gdced29076, main HEAD) — post-load indexer-finalize memory wall sweep
model: lukealonso/GLM-5.2-NVFP4 (450,931,811,448 bytes, 87 shards, index_topk=2048 DSA sparse + indexer_rope_interleave=true + per-layer self_attn.indexer.* weights)
image: glm5-repro:latest-dg (vLLM dev207+gdced29076 main HEAD + flashinfer 0.6.13 + deep_gemm 2.5.0 transplanted)
fleet: 4x DGX Spark GB10 (nodes s1/s2/s3/swork on a private LAN + RDMA fabric), 119-121 GiB unified RAM/node, TP=4
patches_applied: patch_sparse_gate (A: add FLASHINFER_MLA_SPARSE to sm_121 else-branch; B: open supports_compute_capability major==10 -> in (10,12))

what_works.build: latest vLLM main HEAD builds from source for sm_121 via eugr/spark-vllm-docker --rebuild-vllm --rebuild-flashinfer (proven on 5.0).
what_works.deep_gemm: ABSENT in the from-source latest image; transplanted deep_gemm 2.5.0 dir (compiled _C.so) from glm5-repro:t2 -> imports clean (both images share torch 2.11.0+cu130 + flashinfer 0.6.13). Verified `import deep_gemm` = 2.5.0 + fp8_mqa_logits symbol on all 4 ranks every launch.
what_works.dsa_sparse_backend_selects: On latest vLLM the attention selector (vllm/platforms/cuda.py::_get_backend_priorities) hard-branches on device_capability.major==10 (Blackwell); GB10=sm_121 (major=12) hit the else-branch which only offered FLASHMLA_SPARSE (needs uncompiled vllm._flashmla_C). patch_sparse_gate (2 edits) opens FLASHINFER_MLA_SPARSE on sm_121. With it, rank0 logs 'Using FLASHINFER_MLA_SPARSE attention backend' + validates (head_size 576, qk_nope 128, index_topk 2048, fp8 KV all pass). REAL latest-vLLM porting finding.
what_works.weights_load: All 87 shards load to 106.19 GiB resident/node over NFS (~1040s head). Reaches the DEEPSEEK_V32_INDEXER kv-cache setup step every time.

the_wall: POST-LOAD: at the DEEPSEEK_V32_INDEXER kv-cache setup (the DSA indexer's SECOND KV cache + DeepGEMM workspace, allocated OUTSIDE vLLM's gpu_memory_utilization pool) + the post-load profile_run memory-profiling dummy forward, the two WORKER nodes (s2/s3) exhaust physical RAM and NVRM NV_ERR_NO_MEMORY @ mem_desc.c -> pinned-NVRM zombie (ssh banner-starve). Head (s1) loops shm_broadcast 'No available shared memory broadcast block in 60s' waiting on the dead workers; port 8000 never binds. Node asymmetry: s2/s3 (worker ranks) die, s1(head)+swork survive — the documented thinner-worker-node finalize-OOM.

config_sweep.evidence: pool eats ~117.8GiB leaving ~1.2GiB physical out-of-pool; indexer 2nd-KV alloc squeezed; 1 NVRM on s1, parked 40min on indexer line.

pool 107.1GiB -> only ~0.9GiB in-pool; py-spy showed worker BLOCKED in determine_available_memory->profile_run->_dummy_sampler_run->all_gather_into_tensor (NCCL); profile_run dummy-forward STARVED; 7 NVRM on s1 secondary.
loaded cleanly, reached DEEPSEEK_V32_INDEXER, head shm_broadcast loop; s2 + s3 each 2 NVRM NV_ERR_NO_MEMORY; s1+swork 0.

load 87/87 -> 106.19 GiB resident, reached DEEPSEEK_V32_INDEXER kv-setup, head shm_broadcast loop (1->3 over 3min), :8000 never bound; s2 logged 25 NVRM in the wedged boot; flight recorders on s2/s3 froze at avail ~1290 MiB. The balance point (0.95 = more out-of-pool than 0.99, more in-pool than 0.90) + minimal batched-8 forward STILL cannot fit the indexer 2nd-KV + DeepGEMM workspace out-of-pool on the worker nodes.

config_sweep.run: run#21 max-model-len lever (the ONE untried axis)
config_sweep.evidence: The entire prior sweep held max_model_len=2048 FIXED. At index_topk=2048 the DSA indexer's OUT-of-pool 2nd-KV scales with max_model_len, so 2048->512 cuts that indexer-KV ~4x — the one lever that directly shrinks the actual out-of-pool allocation rather than juggling the util split. RESULT: IDENTICAL wall. Load 87/87 -> 106.19 GiB resident (1020.71s weights, 1026.6s total), DSA FLASHINFER_MLA_SPARSE selected, deep_gemm 2.5.0, mem_bypass fired (free=120.7G req=124.1G) — then at 'Setting kv cache block size to 64 for DEEPSEEK_V32_INDEXER backend' the head EngineCore began the shm_broadcast.py:705 60s loop (reached 4 loops, :8000 NEVER bound); s1(head) logged 28 NVRM NV_ERR_NO_MEMORY this boot; s2(rank1)+s3(rank2) banner-timeout = pinned-NVRM memory-wedged; swork(rank3, fattest) survived 0 NVRM. The ~4x smaller indexer-KV did NOT free enough physical RAM on the thin worker ranks. Measured 2026-06-21T01:45Z.

verdict: GLM-5.2-NVFP4 DSA-sparse DOES NOT pass the post-load DEEPSEEK_V32_INDEXER finalize at TP=4 on these 119 GiB GB10 nodes on latest vLLM, across the FULL FIVE-config sweep: util (0.99/0.90/0.95) x forward-size (batched 256/8) x KV (in-pool & decoupled fp8 1e9) x max_model_len (2048/512). The build is fine, deep_gemm transplant is fine, the DSA sparse backend SELECTS and the weights LOAD (106.19 GiB resident) — the failure is a real physical-RAM wall: the DSA indexer's second KV cache + DeepGEMM workspace allocate out-of-pool and exceed what the worker ranks (s2/s3) have left after 106 GiB of weights. Even cutting max_model_len 4x (the only knob that directly shrinks that out-of-pool indexer-KV, not just the util split) did not free enough. This is the same class of wall GLM-5.1-DSA hit (references/glm51-dsa-indexer-coherence.md) and is NOT a tunable on 4x119GiB. NONE MEASURED for 5.2 decode tok/s — honest, because no coherent serve was ever achieved. The path forward needs MORE physical RAM per worker (a fatter-node fleet) or an upstream change that shrinks/shares the DSA indexer's out-of-pool footprint — not a launch flag.
contrast: GLM-5.0-NVFP4 (DENSE path, index_topk=0, patch_dense_mla makes the indexer a no-op) SERVES + DECODES COHERENTLY + BENCHES on the SAME latest image (11.1 tok/s, France->Paris byte-identical to anchor). So the build is proven; the 5.2 failure is cleanly isolated to the DSA-sparse indexer's out-of-pool finalize footprint, NOT the latest-vLLM stack.
